chemistry.product: CO2, p-benzoquinone, hydroquinone, NO2-, NO3-, NH4+
chemistry.scope_summary: Continuous fixed-bed photoreactor study of 4-nitrophenol degradation using TiO2 supported on glass beads. Rate determining step is reaction between •OH radicals and photoadsorbed 4-NP molecules. Main products are CO2 (mineralization) with small amounts of p-benzoquinone and hydroquinone. Kinetics follow Langmuir-Hinshelwood model.

optimization_table.key_findings: Langmuir-Hinshelwood model satisfactorily fitted experimental results

figures.caption: Figure 2. Micrograph (a) and EDAX (b) mapping of a layer of TiO2 supported on a glass bead. The quantitative determination of the substrate in the inlet and outlet solution was performed by means of a 2200 UV-Vis Shimadzu spectrophotometer at a wavelength of 315 nm by means of a multipoint calibration method. translation_logic.batch_limitation: No batch baseline provided for comparison
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous operation with precise residence time control and steady-state kinetics
translation_logic.what_changed: Implementation of continuous flow photoreactor system
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: TiO2 photocatalyst and UV light source
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous operation reduces handling of intermediate products

figure_descriptions: Micrograph and EDAX mapping of TiO2 supported on glass bead

conflicts: Duplicate figure entries in pass 4 results
notes: This is a photocatalytic degradation study in continuous flow mode. No batch baseline is provided for comparison. The study focuses on kinetic modeling using Langmuir-Hinshelwood approach. Key limitation is lack of yield/conversion data and optimization table details.
